canonical_filename.mechanism: Fixed filename SKILL.md required within each named skill directory (SKILLS_FILENAME constant)

description.mechanism: YAML frontmatter key: description (required — empty string triggers MissingField error; max 1024 chars enforced after whitespace normalization)

disable_model_invocation.mechanism: openai.yaml companion file key: policy.allow_implicit_invocation (optional bool; false disables implicit/auto invocation; defaults to true when absent)

display_name.mechanism: YAML frontmatter key: name (optional — falls back to parent directory name if absent or empty; max 64 chars enforced after whitespace normalization)

global_scope.mechanism: Skills directory at $HOME/.agents/skills/ or the user config layer's skills/ subdir ($CODEX_HOME/skills, deprecated but kept for backward compatibility) (SkillScope::User)

project_scope.mechanism: Skills directory at <project_config_folder>/skills/ (SkillScope::Repo) or any .agents/skills/ directory between project root and cwd (monorepo traversal)

shared_scope.mechanism: Admin scope at /etc/codex/skills on Unix (SkillScope::Admin) — machine-level shared skills readable by all local users; injected via System config layer

providerExtensions.name: Short Description
providerExtensions.description: A condensed version of the skill description. Declared in SKILL.md frontmatter under metadata.short-description (YAML key, hyphenated). Also available as interface.short_description in the openai.yaml companion file. Truncated to 1024 chars. Used for compact display contexts where the full description is too long.

providerExtensions.name: openai.yaml Companion Metadata File
providerExtensions.description: An optional companion file at <skill_dir>/agents/openai.yaml that provides UI and policy metadata separate from SKILL.md frontmatter. Contains three top-level sections: interface (display_name, short_description, icon_small, icon_large, brand_color, default_prompt), dependencies (tools list with type, value, description, transport, command, url fields), and policy (allow_implicit_invocation, products). The file is optional — missing it is not an error. Path resolution for icon assets enforces that paths must be relative and under an assets/ subdirectory of the skill dir.

providerExtensions.name: Skill Interface Icons
providerExtensions.description: Skills may declare small and large icons via openai.yaml interface.icon_small and interface.icon_large. Values must be relative paths under the skill's assets/ directory (absolute paths and path traversal via .. are rejected). Used for UI display.

providerExtensions.name: Skill Brand Color
providerExtensions.description: A brand color for the skill declared as interface.brand_color in openai.yaml. Must be a valid #RRGGBB hex string (exactly 7 chars). Used for UI theming of the skill card.

providerExtensions.name: Default Prompt
providerExtensions.description: A default prompt for the skill declared as interface.default_prompt in openai.yaml. Max 1024 chars. Provides a pre-filled starting prompt when a user invokes the skill from a UI that supports it.

providerExtensions.name: Tool Dependencies
providerExtensions.description: Skills can declare tool dependencies via openai.yaml dependencies.tools (a list). Each dependency has: type (required, max 64 chars), value (required, max 1024 chars), description (optional, max 1024 chars), transport (optional, max 64 chars), command (optional, max 1024 chars), url (optional, max 1024 chars). Used to declare what external tools or MCP servers the skill requires.

providerExtensions.name: Product Gating
providerExtensions.description: Skills can be restricted to specific OpenAI products via openai.yaml policy.products (a list of Product values). When products is non-empty, the skill is only shown/used when the active product matches the restriction list. When products is empty, the skill is available for all products. Filtering is applied at load time via filter_skill_load_outcome_for_product().

providerExtensions.name: System Scope (Embedded Skills Cache)
providerExtensions.description: A fourth scope beyond Repo/User/Admin. System-scoped skills are embedded by Codex itself and cached under $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system (SkillScope::System). This scope does NOT follow symlinks (unlike Repo, User, and Admin scopes). Parse errors for system-scoped skills are silently suppressed (not added to SkillLoadOutcome.errors). Skills are sorted Repo > User > System > Admin by priority.

providerExtensions.name: Plugin Scope
providerExtensions.description: A plugin extension mechanism where additional skill roots are injected externally via plugin_skill_roots (Vec<PathBuf> from codex_utils_plugins). Plugin skills are assigned SkillScope::User scope and receive a namespaced name prefix (plugin_namespace_for_skill_path). This allows third-party plugins to contribute skills without modifying config files.

providerExtensions.name: Monorepo Skill Discovery
providerExtensions.description: For Repo-scoped skills, codex walks all directories between the detected project root and the current working directory, collecting any .agents/skills/ subdirectory found along the path. Project root is determined by scanning ancestors for project_root_markers (configurable, defaults to standard VCS/build markers). MAX_SCAN_DEPTH=6 limits traversal depth within each skills root; MAX_SKILLS_DIRS_PER_ROOT=2000 caps total dirs scanned per root.

providerExtensions.name: Skill Deduplication
providerExtensions.description: After loading all roots, skills with duplicate SKILL.md paths are deduplicated — only the first occurrence (highest-priority scope) is kept. Skills are then sorted by scope priority (Repo > User > System > Admin), then by name, then by path.
